mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp, or the signal loses predictive power during non-recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand and residential investment, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.04, or housing market decoupling from broader equity performance invalidates the earnings transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index above 0.5% during periods of rising inflation confirms robust supply-side capacity that supports equity valuations, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth > 0.5% coincides with negative 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of high-inflation observations, indicating supply growth fails to offset monetary tightening pressures.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 1.0% signals severe demand weakness that forces immediate portfolio de-risking, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in > 50% of quarters where GDPC1 growth falls below 1.0%, or the signal shows zero predictive accuracy in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates combined with declining nonfarm payrolls indicate a policy-induced economic slowdown that compresses profit margins and equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combination of rising rates and falling payrolls fails to predict negative 60-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10), or labor market resilience decouples equity performance from monetary policy.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery phase, a monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 4.5% confirms labor market normalization that supports risk appetite, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE declines below 4.5% coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns in > 55% of recovery-phase observations, indicating labor normalization fails to drive equity upside.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month drop in industrial production exceeding 0.4% signals acute supply-side contraction that forces immediate corporate earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns are positive in > 60% of instances where INDPRO drops > 0.4%, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.
